pr.title: Harden workflow GitHub context handling
pr.body:
<!-- Keep this PR as draft until it is ready for review. -->

<!-- AI/LLM agents: be concise and specific. Do not check the box below. -->

HUMAN:

This PR hardens the issue-opened workflow by passing GitHub context values through environment variables before shell use.

- [ ] A human has tested these changes.

AGENT:

---

## Why

Part of the cross-repo fix for OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#3371. Workflow `run:` blocks should not interpolate attacker-influenced GitHub context or derived step outputs directly into shell scripts.

## Summary

- Moves issue number and good-first-issue precheck outputs into `env:` before using them in shell.
- Updates summary shell logic to reference environment variables instead of direct `${{ ... }}` interpolation.

## Issue Number

Fixes OpenHands/software-agent-sdk#3371

## How to Test

- `python` + `PyYAML` validation over all changed workflow/action YAML files across the audited repositories: `validated changed yaml files: 33`
- Repository scanner confirmed: `remaining suspicious run blocks: 0`
- `git diff --check` across all audited repositories

## Video/Screenshots

Not applicable; workflow hardening only.

## Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Docs / chore

## Notes

This PR was created by an AI agent (OpenHands) on behalf of the user.
